title: Yellow Peaches in the Boland

seo.title: Yellow Peaches in the Boland - Marinda Kook

html:
I was lucky enough to buy these peaches just outside Robertson at a wonderful farm stall. They still have their pips in – so they're not pitted!

What I do with the peaches you can do with any dried fruit. It's delicious with meat, in the morning just as it is or with your muesli, or as a dessert with custard. Healthy and good for the stomach!There isn't really a recipe as such, you just toss it all into a pot.

  1. 6 cups fruit
  2. 3 cups water (should cover three quarters of the fruit)
  3. 1 cup sugar (you can decide how sweet you want it)
  4. 2 pieces stick cinnamon

NOW

  • pour the water and sugar into a pot on the stove. Cook over medium heat until the sugar has dissolved.
  • now put the fruit and cinnamon stick into the sugar syrup and put the lid on
  • cook until the fruit is soft but not mushy (roughly 20 minutes, it depends on the fruit you're using)

Eat it cold or warm. It's a side dish at a meal, an early-morning breakfast, or a dessert with custard. It keeps well and stays healthy. It lasts a long time in the fridge in a container.
